note: Title of the manuscript (τυπικόν) on f. 71r

The text falls broadly into three parts: 1) general rules on how to celebrate services. This section occupies the first seven folia of Quire one; 2) synaxarion, listing a selection of fixed daily celebrations, from the beginning of the liturgical year on 1 September until 31 August (from the second half of Quire one to the third to last folio of Quire five); 3) triodion, with the movable cycle, running from the Sunday of the Publican and the Pharisee to Easter Monday (the last two folia of Quire five until the end of Quire seven). The final part of the manuscript—from Easter Monday to All Saints’ Sunday (the first Sunday after Pentecost)—is missing. I have calculated that approximately two quires are now lost. All in all, this means that the Typikon originally consisted of approximately 70 folia (Rossetto)

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
